title: #113 Avatar Rewrite — Mockup v1
textSample:
Bug #113 — Avatar Rewrite Mockup v1

Target: replace QLabel+setPixmap+border-radius approach with a custom QWidget that paints via QPainter directly. Rounded-square, 8px radius, 36×36 logical. Qt's paintEvent rendering pipeline is inherently DPR-aware — no pixmap DPR juggling, no QLabel anchoring surprises, no QSS border-radius clip gaps.

screenshot: C:\CODEX PG\CODEX Claude UX Mockup Review\CODEX rendered screenshots\02_action_bar_mockup_v1.png
title: PG — Unified Action Bar Mockup v1 (#107 + #110)
textSample:
Unified Action Bar — #107 + #110 bundled fix (Mockup v1)

Target: move all contextual action buttons (PASS, FAIL, SKIP, Next →) out of the scroll area and into the pane-level action bar at the bottom. One row, always reachable, state-dependent content. Fixes #110 (buttons scroll out of view on tall rows) and #107 (two-row footer on checklist steps). Widths shown: 430 px (MIN_SIZE, current floor) and 360 px (below wrap breakpoint) to prove the layout holds across the pane's size range.

ARCHITECTURAL CHANGE

Before: In-row _actions_host (PASS/FAIL/SKIP on the active row, inside the scroll area) plus a separate ChecklistStepView._footer (summary + Next →) below the scroll area, plus the pane-level button_row (Cancel / Pause / ← Back) at the bottom. Three horizontal action surfaces, two of them inside the scroll region.

After: One pane-level action bar hosts all buttons. _actions_host is removed from _ChecklistRow entirely. ChecklistStepView._footer dissolves — its Next button moves to the pane bar, and its summary text moves to a new thin summary strip above the action bar (always visible once any item is answered, per recommendation — see Option A vs B below).

title: Panda Gallery — Checklist View Mockup v2
textSample:
Checklist view — mockup v2
Three pane widths + a live resizable demo. Unified list, no walled cards, width-clamped.
WIDTH CONSTRAINTS — CLAUDE CODE MUST ENFORCE THESE IN CODE
Hard minimum: pane width ≥ 280 px. Apply via setMinimumWidth(280) on the instruction-pane dock widget. Below this, Pass/Fail/Skip collide with the label regardless of wrapping.
Wrap breakpoint: at pane width ≤ 360 px, the active row drops its button row below the label (separate line, right-aligned). Implemented via a layout switch keyed off the pane's current width in resizeEvent.
Above 360 px: buttons sit inline on the right of the active row.
Title bar: step name truncates with ellipsis; step count (2 / 4) never truncates.
Live resizable demo — grab the bottom-right corner and drag

screenshot: C:\CODEX PG\CODEX Claude UX Mockup Review\CODEX rendered screenshots\05_dialog_sizing_v1.png
title: #129 Sizing rule mockup v1 — Settings dialog
textSample:
#129 sizing rule — Settings dialog mockup v1

Visual target for the four-invariant sizing rule. Each scenario shows the Testing — Settings dialog (Pane tab) at a different width. Buttons stay intact across all widths; text wraps at the narrow end; gap between buttons is constant 10px. Default width on first open = floor × 1.15.

The four invariants
Buttons all visible. Every button in the active cluster fully rendered. None clipped, none hidden.
Inter-button spacing fixed. 10px gap between buttons. Never compressed. Window can't shrink past the point where compression would be required.
Text never clipped. Labels word-wrap freely (window grows vertically). No horizontal truncation.
Multi-line inputs ≥ 2 lines. Any QTextEdit has min-height = 2 lines of rendered font + padding.
Settings dialog — three widths
AT FLOOR — 320 px
smallest valid size

screenshot: C:\CODEX PG\CODEX Claude UX Mockup Review\CODEX rendered screenshots\07_instruction_pane_redesign_v1.png
title: Panda Gallery — Instruction Pane redesign v1 (closes #107 #108 #110 #111)
textSample:
Instruction Pane — redesign v1

Target file: instruction_pane.py · Closes: #107 (two-row footer waste) · #108 (oversized FAIL textarea) · #110 (in-row PASS/FAIL/SKIP scroll out of view) · #111 (cramped/not modern)

DESIGN PRINCIPLES
Action bar is always reachable. PASS / FAIL / SKIP / Next live at the pane bottom, sibling to Cancel / Pause / Back. They never scroll with content.
Breathing room. Root padding 20/22, section gap 14, button height 32, all uniform. No squished corners.
FAIL textarea is capped. min 64, max 84, internal scroll on overflow. Never dominates the pane.
One content area per step kind. Single-expected shows body + EXPECTED + filler. Checklist shows body + scrolling list + summary strip. Mutually exclusive.
Summary strip is subtle but present. Right-aligned, muted text, one line above the action bar. Counts use the existing dot colors.
No new colors. Everything pulled from styles.py.

title: Pane action bar v2 — #124 + #125 bundle — mockup v1
textSample:
Instruction Pane action bar v2 — #124 + #125 bundle

One redesign wave, two bugs closed. #124: two-row action bar so the pane can shrink below 560 px. #125: new kind: "action" step renders an Acknowledge button instead of PASS/FAIL for navigation-only steps. Three variants of the Acknowledge button are shown side-by-side (§6) — pick one before we draft the Claude Code prompt. Colors from styles.py: peach #e8a87c, green #5ab87a, bg #14141f / #1a1a2e, border #2a2a3e.

§1 Observation step — kind: "observation" / legacy expectedbaseline — what most steps look like today

Single-row layout is fine above the 420 px breakpoint. Below that, the action bar wraps to two rows. Navigation cluster (Cancel / Pause / Back) goes left; outcome cluster (PASS / FAIL) goes right.

title: Panda Gallery — Pane-level FAIL panel mockup v1 (Phase B, bug #87)
textSample:
Pane-level FAIL panel — mockup v1
Phase B of bug #87. Single-expected steps: replace the action row (PASS / FAIL) with an inline FAIL panel when the user taps FAIL, so the user can type a note and commit without leaving the pane. Visual language reuses the checklist-row FAIL panel's QSS verbatim — #checklistFailPanel, #checklistFailPanelEdit, #checklistBtnPanelCancel, #checklistBtnPanelSave.
THREE STATES

State 1 — Default. Body + EXPECTED + action row (PASS / FAIL). Unchanged from v3.75.6.

State 2 — FAIL panel open. Action row replaced with an inline panel: red-tinted container, "DESCRIBE WHAT FAILED" header, multi-line textarea, CANCEL + SAVE FAIL button row. Bottom-of-pane Cancel / Pause / ← Back row stays visible (pane-level actions).

State 3 — Post-save. Result written to results_latest.json, auto-advance to the next step (or to summary on last step). Identical to PASS advance.

A dedicated swatch board at the bottom shows every button state.

textSample:
#132 — Review dialog: 30% size reduction + anchored to pane

Two changes: (1) shrink the preview image cap from 800×600 to 560×420 (exactly 30% each dimension), and (2) position the dialog flush against the Instruction Pane instead of centered on the main window. Fallback to center-on-main when no pane is visible.

screenshot: C:\CODEX PG\CODEX Claude UX Mockup Review\CODEX rendered screenshots\13_single_expected_mockup_v1.png
title: Panda Gallery — Single-Expected Step Mockup v1
textSample:
Single-expected step layout — mockup v1
Fix for G1/G4 (wasted vertical space) + G2 (button style) + G3 (narrow-resize button truncation).
WHAT THIS MOCKUP LOCKS IN
Body gets NO vertical stretch. Uses flex: 0 0 auto + max-height: 40%. Only scrolls if content actually overflows.
Spacer absorbs empty space. A flex: 1 1 auto element between body and EXPECTED pushes EXPECTED + button row to the bottom of the pane. Content top, void middle, buttons bottom. Predictable.
All bottom-bar buttons are outlined — no solid fills. PASS becomes green-outline (was solid green). FAIL stays amber-outline. Cancel/Pause/Back stay neutral-outline. Entire pane speaks one visual vocabulary.
PASS and FAIL get setMinimumWidth(76) and setMinimumWidth(72). Prevents the G3 narrow-resize truncation.
Pane MIN_SIZE width bumps from 320 → 380. Five outlined buttons at their floor widths + margins need 380 to fit without overlap.
1. Wasted vertical space — before vs after
BEFORE (CURRENT V3.66) — G1 / G4

screenshot: C:\CODEX PG\CODEX Claude UX Mockup Review\CODEX rendered screenshots\14_single_expected_mockup_v2.png
title: Panda Gallery — Single-Expected Step Mockup v2 (filler placement)
textSample:
Single-expected step — filler placement v2
Choosing where the flex: 1 spacer goes when body content is short vs. medium. Informed by live-testing screenshots showing the v1 mockup only drew the short-body case.
THE CONFLICT THAT SURFACED

v1 mockup (shipped in v3.66): spacer between body and EXPECTED. EXPECTED pinned to bottom of pane, just above button row. Works great for short body text.

Live testing with B1 / B2 (medium-length body): the spacer creates a visible gap between body and EXPECTED. The user reads this as "wasted space" identical to the original G1/G4 bug — we just relocated the void.

Root question: where should the absorbed void go — above EXPECTED, or below EXPECTED?

Short body (A1 — "Look at the pane")

screenshot: C:\CODEX PG\CODEX Claude UX Mockup Review\CODEX rendered screenshots\15_stats_row_mic_bar_mockup_v1.png
title: PG 3c — stats-row MicLevelBar swap mockup v1
textSample:
Phase 3c — stats-row MicLevelBar swap

Mockup v1. Target: replace the single-QLabel _stats_lbl in InstructionPane with a fixed-height row that renders a MicLevelBar during AudioState.ACTIVE and a text dot in SILENT / DISCONNECTED / FAILED. Row height pinned at 30 px across all states.

Stats-row text decision for ACTIVE state (Option A, recommended). During ACTIVE, drop the word “Capturing” from the stats text. The animating bar communicates “capturing” more directly than the word. Non-ACTIVE states retain the full phrase because the dot colour carries less information than the bar does.
   ACTIVE text: Frames: NNNN · MM:SS
   Non-ACTIVE text: Capturing · Frames: NNNN · MM:SS (DISCONNECTED overrides with its own copy.)
Width: 430 px (pane MIN_SIZE floor)

Hardest layout case. Pane inner width = 430 − 32 = 398 px. Bar must hold its 200 px minimum; text column absorbs the rest via flex: 1 with ellipsis on overflow.

screenshot: C:\CODEX PG\CODEX Claude UX Mockup Review\CODEX rendered screenshots\16_v3_71_ui_vocabulary.png
title: Where each part of the pane lives
textSample:
Where each part of the pane lives

Yellow labels point at the real regions. Three states side by side.

Quick reference
HELP
The ? circle, top right. Opens a key reference.
PROGRESS
"Step X of Y" text, top left.
TITLE
The orange heading of the step.
INSTRUCTIONS
The plain text block that tells you what to do. Scrolls if long.
QUESTION LIST
The rows with colored dots. The active one has an orange left stripe.
SUMMARY STRIP
The thin "X pass · X fail · X skip" line that shows up after you answer one question.
ACTION BAR
The 6 buttons at the very bottom: Cancel, Pause, Back, PASS, FAIL, SKIP.
FAIL PANEL
The red-bordered textbox that opens when you press FAIL.

title: Panda Gallery v4.0 — Design Mockup Index
textSample:
PANDAGallery Design Mockups
v4.0 · Week 1 shell mockup · iteration 1
SCREENS
The main shell in context — all three modules, empty-state, loupe. Click to open full-window.
PANDA
Splash / startup
First-impression screen shown while the app boots. Atmospheric glow, loading bar, version footer.
SHELL
Library · patient selected
Main workspace. Patient list left, thumbnail grid center, properties right, filmstrip bottom. The default screen after launch.
SHELL
Library · empty state
No patient selected. Three-step onboarding: select patient → import images → arrange & present. Addresses bug #114.
SHELL
Library · loupe (image zoomed)
Single image editing. Tool strip appears, right panel shifts to Adjust / History / Annotations. Canvas darkens to pure black.
SHELL
Arrange · unified canvas
Template + freeform consolidated. Drag from image library on left onto the arrangement sheet. Saved arrangements reusable.
SHELL
Present · dual-monitor
First-class patient-facing view. Control monitor has chrome, patient monitor is zero-chrome mirror. The differentiated feature.
SHELL
REFERENCE
Deep-dive documentation: every menu, every toolbar, every keyboard shortcut, in one place.
FILE
 
Ctrl+O Open
Ctrl

title: Panda Gallery v4.0 — Design Index
textSample:
PANDAGallery — Design Index
v4.0 · complete mockup library · updated 2026-04-23
17
mockups
7
primary screens
5
studies & references
palette locked
Version v4.0
Target ship 2026-07-23
Palette · Segoe UI · peach
01 · PRIMARY SCREENS

The screens a dentist moves through in a typical day. These are the core app surfaces — every other screen exists to support what happens here.

GORGEOUS
Editor · Adjust panel
Single-image loupe with full Lightroom-style adjustment panel, tool strip, filmstrip, and histogram. The screen where actual clinical work happens.
v4_0_edit_image_mockup.html
Open ↗
Splash screen
App launch — brand-forward with peach gradient PANDA wordmark, v4.0 version badge, loading status.
v4_0_splash_mockup.html
Open ↗
Main shell · Library
Proposed: Patient list · thumbnail grid · filter panel · filmstrip. The screen a dentist opens to first thing every morning.
v4_0_shell_mockup_v1_library.html
Open ↗
NEW
Library · empty states
First launch (no patients), patient list with no selection, patient selected but zero images. Three states in one file.
v4_0_library_empty_mockup.html
Open ↗
NEW
Arrange · canvas (populated)
Full-mouth series with 16 of 18 slots mounted. Image palette

title: Panda Gallery v4.0 — Palette & Typography
textSample:
← Design index / palette & typography
PANDAGallery — Design Tokens
Atomic design layer · colors from styles.py · type stack from app config
COLOR · SURFACE
Five-step dark navy scale. All surface colors in the app come from this scale — no ad-hoc hex values in component code.
canvas
#14141f
Deepest — viewport background, scroll areas
chrome
#161625
Title bar, menu bar, toolbar, status bar
pane
#1a1a2e
Main window, side panes, dialogs
pane-raised
#22223a
Buttons, inputs, hover state base
pane-selected
#2a2a4e
Selected state, pressed state
COLOR · BORDER & DIVIDER
border-soft
#232336
Subtle section dividers, row borders
border
#2a2a3e
Default component borders
border-hover
#3a3a4e
Hover state for borders, scrollbar handles
COLOR · TEXT
Aa Bb Cc 123
text
#e0ddd5
Primary cream — body text, labels
Aa Bb Cc 123
text-muted
#888888
Secondary info, counts, captions
Aa Bb Cc 123
text-dim
#555555
Disabled state, tertiary info, placeholders
COLOR · ACCENT (SIGNATURE)
Peach / coral scale used for selection, focus, brand accent. The logo wordmark uses the 3-stop gradient across accent-hover → accent → accent-ink.
accent-light
#f0b98c
Wordmark highlight, subtle glow top-stop
accent
#e8a87c
Signatu

screenshot: C:\CODEX PG\CODEX Claude UX Mockup Review\CODEX rendered screenshots\27_v4_0_v4_0_right_panel_study.png
title: Panda Gallery v4.0 — Right Panel Study
textSample:
PANDAGallery — Right Panel Study
Lightroom sliders · collapsible sections · tool inventory with rationale
1 · LIGHTROOM SLIDER — ANATOMY

Your lightroom_slider.py already implements most of what LR does — fine/coarse arrow keys, scroll wheel, inline numeric entry, double-click-label-to-reset, drag tooltips, two-tier rendering. This study is about visual polish plus one piece of behavior worth adding (the center detent + bidirectional fill for bipolar sliders).

Exposure
+0.60
label · double-click resets
center detent (0)
thumb · cream
fill from center (peach, 80%)
value · mono · click to edit
Compare bipolar vs unipolar fill behavior — the fill direction communicates the sign of the change at a glance:
Shadows
−100
Highlights
−82
Contrast
0
Clarity
+42
Strength
65%
↑ unipolar (0-100) · fill from left, no center detent
ALREADY IN LIGHTROOMSLIDER
Fine-step (arrow keys), coarse-step (shift+arrow), scroll wheel adjust, inline numeric editing, double-click label to reset, drag tooltip, dragStarted / dragEnded for two-tier rendering, disabled-state palette.
WORTH ADDING
Center detent + bidirectional fill for bipolar sliders — ~10 lines in paintEvent. Track tick at center, fill draws from

title: Panda Gallery v4.0 — Toolbar & Panel Reference
textSample:
← Design index / toolbars & panels
PANDAGallery — Toolbars & Panels
Every clickable control in the shell — top toolbar, tool strip, right-panel tabs
TOP TOOLBAR
Persistent strip below the menu bar. Always visible. Canvas-bound actions are disabled when no image is loaded.
📂
Open
↶
Undo
↷
Redo
⊕
Zoom+
⊖
Zoom−
⊡
Fit
↑
Export
⎙
Print
Open
Open a single image file (Ctrl+O) — quick edit without a patient association.
Undo / Redo
Per-image edit history (Ctrl+Z / Ctrl+Y). Separate from Back / Forward navigation.
Zoom+ / Zoom−
Step zoom (Ctrl+= / Ctrl+−). Mouse wheel also zooms on the canvas directly.
Fit
Fit image to viewport (Home). First thing you reach for after loading an image.
Export
Export with annotations baked in. PNG / JPEG / TIFF / BMP.
Print
Print preview dialog — lets user pick printer, paper size, fit-to-page.
TOOL STRIP · DRAWING & ANNOTATION
Vertical column on the left of the canvas. Visible only in loupe (single-image) mode. Single-key shortcuts match Lightroom / Photoshop convention.
